# 🖼️ Image (185)

From a blank canvas to a billion pixels of detail, these tools paint, repair, and reimagine. Whether you're prompting a masterpiece or rescuing a blurry scan, the state of the art lives here.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## Generation

Prompt-to-picture models that render photoreal, painterly, or impossible images in seconds

- [Zheng-Chong/CatVTON](https://github.com/Zheng-Chong/CatVTON) — ⭐ 1,681 · `Python` · Paper Code — CatVTON is an efficient virtual try-on diffusion model designed for fashion applications. ## Editing

Inpaint, outpaint, and instruction-edit pixels with a sentence instead of a brush

<details>
<summary>Show 23 repos</summary>

- [upscayl/upscayl](https://github.com/upscayl/upscayl) — ⭐ 45,138 · `TypeScript` · Application — A free and open-source AI image upscaler that locally enhances image resolution on Linux, macOS, and Windows, serving as an alternative to paid or online services.

- [allenk/GeminiWatermarkTool](https://github.com/allenk/GeminiWatermarkTool) — ⭐ 2,317 · `C++` · Application — This is a tool designed to remove visible and invisible (SynthID) watermarks from images and videos generated by Gemini and VEO AI models. - [wiltodelta/remove-ai-watermarks](https://github.com/wiltodelta/remove-ai-watermarks) — ⭐ 1,852 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This tool provides a CLI and library to remove both visible and invisible AI watermarks from images, including those from Gemini, Midjourney, and SynthID, by using diffusion models and also preserves facial details during processing.

- [yossTheDev/removerized](https://github.com/yossTheDev/removerized) — ⭐ 1,114 · `TypeScript` · Application — Removerized is an AI Image Toolkit that provides functionalities such as background removal and image upscaling, running entirely in the browser for privacy and offline usage.

## Restoration

Denoise, deblur, and revive old or damaged photos to crisp clarity

- [EutropicAI/Final2x](https://github.com/EutropicAI/Final2x) — ⭐ 7,179 · `TypeScript` · Application — Final2x is a cross-platform image super-resolution tool that uses computer vision techniques and a PyTorch backend to enhance image quality. It allows users to upscale images with improved detail and clarity.
- [Hugo-Dz/spritefusion-pixel-snapper](https://github.com/Hugo-Dz/spritefusion-pixel-snapper) — ⭐ 1,979 · `Rust` · CLI Tool — This is a CLI tool designed to snap AI-generated pixel art to a perfect grid, fixing inconsistencies and improving its quality.
- [W2GenAI-Lab/LucidFlux](https://github.com/W2GenAI-Lab/LucidFlux) — ⭐ 1,206 · `Python` · Paper Code — LucidFlux is a research project implementing a large-scale diffusion transformer model for caption-free, photo-realistic image restoration, specifically focusing on super-resolution. It aims to enhance image quality for real-world high-resolution outputs.
- [Kiteretsu77/APISR](https://github.com/Kiteretsu77/APISR) — ⭐ 1,100 · `Python` · Paper Code — APISR is a project focused on enhancing anime quality using super-resolution techniques inspired by professional anime production pipelines. It aims to improve real-world anime visuals.
- [cvlab-stonybrook/DewarpNet](https://github.com/cvlab-stonybrook/DewarpNet) — ⭐ 621 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository contains the code for the academic paper "DewarpNet: Single-Image Document Unwarping With Stacked 3D and 2D Regression Networks" presented at ICCV '19. It focuses on correcting geometric distortions in single-image documents using neural networks.
- [yfyang007/ControlLight](https://github.com/yfyang007/ControlLight) — ⭐ 62 · `Python` · Model Weights — A controllable low-light enhancement model (LoRA) designed to improve visibility in dark images. It aims to preserve the original scene structure, visual content, and fine-grained details during the enhancement process.
- [taco-group/4KLSDB](https://github.com/taco-group/4KLSDB) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Dataset — This repository provides 4KLSDB, a large-scale native-4K dataset and benchmark designed for tasks related to image restoration and generation.
- [wafer-bob/ASASR](https://github.com/wafer-bob/ASASR) — ⭐ 13 · Paper Code — ASASR is the official implementation of a research paper on image super-resolution, designed to transform blurry, low-quality photos into sharp, high-resolution images. It focuses on preventing hallucinated details and improving OCR and segmentation accuracy.

## Segmentation

Pixel-perfect masks and cutouts that isolate any object on command

<details>
<summary>Show 11 repos</summary>

- [roboflow/rf-detr](https://github.com/roboflow/rf-detr) — ⭐ 7,140 · `Python` · Library — RF-DETR is a state-of-the-art model architecture for real-time object detection and instance segmentation developed by Roboflow. It achieves SOTA performance on COCO and is designed for fine-tuning on custom datasets.
- [wasserth/TotalSegmentator](https://github.com/wasserth/TotalSegmentator) — ⭐ 2,715 · `Python` · Library — TotalSegmentator is a Python tool designed for robustly segmenting over 100 significant anatomical structures in medical CT and MR images.
- [uni-medical/SAM-Med3D](https://github.com/uni-medical/SAM-Med3D) — ⭐ 912 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides SAM-Med3D, an efficient general-purpose promptable segmentation model specifically designed for 3D volumetric medical images.
- [tandpfun/wardrobe](https://github.com/tandpfun/wardrobe) — ⭐ 634 · `JavaScript` · Application — This application helps users organize their wardrobe by leveraging a GPT-image model to extract and categorize clothing items from images. It aims to simplify the process of managing and visually organizing a personal collection of clothes.
- [visinf/INSID3](https://github.com/visinf/INSID3) — ⭐ 356 · `Python` · Paper Code — INSID3 is a research project presented at CVPR 2026, demonstrating a training-free method for in-context image segmentation using DINOv3. It provides code for this novel computer vision technique.
- [Luo-Yihao/FaithC](https://github.com/Luo-Yihao/FaithC) — ⭐ 277 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official PyTorch and CUDA implementation for "Faithful Contouring," a research paper presented at CVPR 2026. It focuses on a computer vision technique for accurately delineating object boundaries in images.
- [yunxiangfu2001/SegMAN](https://github.com/yunxiangfu2001/SegMAN) — ⭐ 253 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents SegMAN, a research project implementing a novel semantic segmentation model for images that uses state space models and local attention for omni-scale context modeling.
- [NTU-AI4X/ConceptSeg-R1](https://github.com/NTU-AI4X/ConceptSeg-R1) — ⭐ 212 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project introduces a meta-reinforcement learning approach for generalized concept segmentation in images. It aims to segment abstract, rare, or open-domain concepts by extending traditional object-level segmentation to conceptual levels, using LLM inference states to guide existing segmentation models.
- [dataplayer12/SAM3-TensorRT](https://github.com/dataplayer12/SAM3-TensorRT) — ⭐ 142 · `Cuda` · Library — This repository provides an optimized NVIDIA TensorRT engine for Meta AI's SAM-3 (Segment Anything Model) to enable high-performance inference.
- [WZH0120/SAM3-UNet](https://github.com/WZH0120/SAM3-UNet) — ⭐ 88 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides an adaptation of the Segment Anything Model (SAM) 3, optimized to run on GPUs with limited memory, specifically 6GB, making the advanced image segmentation model more accessible.
- [RADSeg-OVSS/RADSeg](https://github.com/RADSeg-OVSS/RADSeg) — ⭐ 54 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the source code for the RADSeg research paper, focusing on parameter and compute-efficient zero-shot open-vocabulary image segmentation. It offers an encoder that can be used as a drop-in replacement in other models for advanced segmentation tasks.

</details>

## Style Transfer

Repaint any photo in the look of any artist, era, or aesthetic

- [BigStationW/ComfyUi-Untwisting-RoPE](https://github.com/BigStationW/ComfyUi-Untwisting-RoPE) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository offers a training-free style transfer method, "Untwisting RoPE," designed for DiT models, enabling controllable style-aligned generation. It is provided as a custom node and workflow for ComfyUI, with current support for Z Img Turbo.

## Diffusion Tools

ControlNets, LoRAs, samplers, and the toolkit that tames diffusion models

<details>
<summary>Show 40 repos</summary>

- [Comfy-Org/ComfyUI](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI) — ⭐ 112,189 · `Python` · Framework — ComfyUI is a powerful, modular GUI, API, and backend for diffusion models, featuring a graph/nodes interface. It enables users to build and run complex workflows for generative AI tasks, especially with models like Stable Diffusion.
- [lllyasviel/Fooocus](https://github.com/lllyasviel/Fooocus) — ⭐ 48,818 · `Python` · Application — Fooocus is an application focused on simplifying the process of prompting and generating creative content, primarily images. It aims to make AI image generation more accessible and user-friendly, likely leveraging diffusion models.
- [modelscope/DiffSynth-Studio](https://github.com/modelscope/DiffSynth-Studio) — ⭐ 12,442 · `Python` · Application — DiffSynth-Studio is a platform that leverages diffusion models, specifically offering an "Offload Training" feature. This allows users with limited GPU resources to train large models like Qwen-Image or Wan2.2-14B on a single consumer GPU by optimizing VRAM usage.
- [kohya-ss/sd-scripts](https://github.com/kohya-ss/sd-scripts) — ⭐ 7,020 · `Python` · Library — A collection of Python scripts for training and fine-tuning Stable Diffusion models, including ControlNet-LLLite, specifically designed for image generation tasks. It provides tools for customizing and optimizing diffusion models.
- [kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes](https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-KJNodes) — ⭐ 2,654 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — A collection of custom nodes that extend the functionality of ComfyUI, providing additional tools and integrations, including specific nodes for interacting with models like Ideogram 4.
- [SonyResearch/micro\_diffusion](https://github.com/SonyResearch/micro_diffusion) — ⭐ 1,587 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation for research on micro-budget training of large-scale diffusion models. It focuses on methods to train these powerful generative models efficiently with significantly reduced computational resources.
- [hustvl/LightningDiT](https://github.com/hustvl/LightningDiT) — ⭐ 1,489 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents LightningDiT, a method addressing the optimization dilemma in latent diffusion models for image reconstruction and generation. It aims to improve the balance between these two tasks, based on research presented at CVPR 2025.
- [Tavris1/ComfyUI-Easy-Install](https://github.com/Tavris1/ComfyUI-Easy-Install) — ⭐ 1,350 · `Batchfile` · CLI Tool — This repository provides a portable installer for ComfyUI, a popular graphical user interface for Stable Diffusion. It simplifies the setup of ComfyUI across Windows, macOS, and Linux, specifically supporting Nvidia GPUs for enhanced performance.
- [helblazer811/Diffusion-Explorer](https://github.com/helblazer811/Diffusion-Explorer) — ⭐ 1,112 · `Svelte` · Application — An interactive web application providing visualizations that illustrate the geometric intuition behind how diffusion models work.
- [nv-tlabs/PiD](https://github.com/nv-tlabs/PiD) — ⭐ 832 · `Python` · Paper Code — PiD (Pixel Diffusion Decoder) is a system for fast, high-resolution latent decoding using pixel diffusion. It aims to improve 4K image visual quality by addressing issues like color-shifting and supports integration with various diffusion models such as FLUX and Qwen-Image.
- [NVlabs/FastGen](https://github.com/NVlabs/FastGen) — ⭐ 735 · `Python` · Library — NVIDIA FastGen is a library designed to accelerate the generation process from large-scale diffusion models.
- [bytedance/comfyui-lumi-batcher](https://github.com/bytedance/comfyui-lumi-batcher) — ⭐ 566 · `TypeScript` · Plugin/Extension — ComfyUI Lumi Batcher is an extension for ComfyUI that enhances AI creation workflows by enabling batch processing of parameters, allowing users to efficiently test multiple configurations and compare generated visual outputs for AI art.
- [Comfy-Org/workflow\_templates](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates) — ⭐ 495 · `Python` · Boilerplate/Template — This repository provides a collection of pre-configured workflow templates for ComfyUI, a powerful node-based interface for Stable Diffusion and other generative AI models. It helps users quickly set up and execute complex image generation tasks.
- [shootthesound/comfyUI-Realtime-Lora](https://github.com/shootthesound/comfyUI-Realtime-Lora) — ⭐ 478 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This toolkit allows users to train, analyze, and edit LoRA models in real-time directly within ComfyUI, supporting various generative AI models like SDXL, SD 1.5, Flux, and Z-Image. It enables fine-tuning and selective loading of model blocks for customized image generation.
- [chenfengxu714/StreamDiffusionV2](https://github.com/chenfengxu714/StreamDiffusionV2) — ⭐ 478 · `Python` · Application — StreamDiffusionV2 is a live stream application that likely leverages diffusion models for real-time visual generation or processing. It aims to apply diffusion model technology in a streaming context.
- [nova452/ComfyUI-Conditioning-Rebalance](https://github.com/nova452/ComfyUI-Conditioning-Rebalance) — ⭐ 425 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides conditioning optimizer nodes for ComfyUI, offering IP-Adapter-like features for image reference editing with models such as Krea 2 and Ideogram 4, thereby enhancing the overall image generation process.
- [erosDiffusion/ComfyUI-EulerDiscreteScheduler](https://github.com/erosDiffusion/ComfyUI-EulerDiscreteScheduler) — ⭐ 383 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a custom node for ComfyUI that implements the Euler Discrete Scheduler, a specific algorithm used for sampling and generating images within diffusion models. It's designed to be integrated into image generation workflows, particularly those involving LoRA fine-tuned models.
- [hanjq17/Spectrum](https://github.com/hanjq17/Spectrum) — ⭐ 120 · `Python` · Paper Code — This project presents "Spectrum," a research contribution focused on accelerating the sampling process for diffusion models. It proposes an adaptive spectral feature forecasting method to reduce the computational cost and time required for generating high-quality images and videos.
- [kijai/ComfyUI-MemoryVisualization](https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-MemoryVisualization) — ⭐ 90 · `JavaScript` · Plugin/Extension — A custom node for ComfyUI that provides a visualization of memory usage, helping users monitor and optimize their GPU memory when running diffusion models.
- [ali-vilab/DiffusionOPD](https://github.com/ali-vilab/DiffusionOPD) — ⭐ 90 · `Python` · Paper Code — DiffusionOPD presents a unified method for on-policy distillation in diffusion models. It allows a single student model to learn from multiple expert diffusion teachers, enabling it to master various text-to-image tasks without experiencing catastrophic forgetting.
- [google/hackable\_diffusion](https://github.com/google/hackable_diffusion) — ⭐ 74 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides resources for training and learning about diffusion models, offering code and materials for understanding and building new applications based on this technology.
- [shootthesound/ComfyUI-Angelo](https://github.com/shootthesound/ComfyUI-Angelo) — ⭐ 66 · `JavaScript` · Plugin/Extension — ComfyUI-Angelo is a ComfyUI node that offers click-to-refine and smart inpainting capabilities for image generation, supporting FLUX 2 Klein models. It enables users to quickly refine generated images through direct interaction for localized or whole-image edits.
- [kijai/ComfyUI-HFRemoteVae](https://github.com/kijai/ComfyUI-HFRemoteVae) — ⭐ 53 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — A ComfyUI node that implements a remote VAE for Hugging Face models, designed to reduce VRAM usage during image and video generation with models like Stable Diffusion and SDXL.
- [AHEKOT/ComfyUI\_HYWorld2](https://github.com/AHEKOT/ComfyUI_HYWorld2) — ⭐ 48 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository contains a custom node for ComfyUI, enhancing its capabilities primarily related to image generation and potentially specific visual effects or world creation within the ComfyUI workflow environment.
- [nregret/Comfyui-Anima-Tools](https://github.com/nregret/Comfyui-Anima-Tools) — ⭐ 40 · `JavaScript` · Plugin/Extension — This is a plugin for ComfyUI that offers an intuitive visual interface for selecting artists and anime characters, helping AI artists quickly generate accurate prompts for two-dimensional AI image creation.
- [gregowahoo/comfyui-workflow-finder](https://github.com/gregowahoo/comfyui-workflow-finder) — ⭐ 36 · `Python` · Application — This tool provides semantic search, graph preview, and 'wild search' capabilities to help users find and manage their ComfyUI workflows.
- [tang-bd/v-grpo](https://github.com/tang-bd/v-grpo) — ⭐ 36 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents V-GRPO, an online reinforcement learning algorithm designed to denoise generative models, particularly diffusion models, achieving state-of-the-art performance and faster execution.
- [SkyShunsuke/InversionAD](https://github.com/SkyShunsuke/InversionAD) — ⭐ 28 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official implementation of InvAD, a method for reconstruction-free anomaly detection using diffusion models. It detects anomalies by identifying deviations from the standard Gaussian distribution in the latent space during the inverse transformation process.
- [viralvfx/ComfyUI-INT4-Fast](https://github.com/viralvfx/ComfyUI-INT4-Fast) — ⭐ 27 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a custom node for ComfyUI that speeds up INT4 model inference by using Tensor Cores. It's designed to enhance the performance of ComfyUI, particularly for models that utilize INT4 quantization.
- [SKBv0/ComfyUI\_ShowMe](https://github.com/SKBv0/ComfyUI_ShowMe) — ⭐ 25 · `JavaScript` · Plugin/Extension — A ComfyUI canvas overlay that allows users to add notes, labels, and highlights to their workflows. It's designed for better organization and explanation of complex generative AI processes.
- [Merserk/ComfyUI-PiD](https://github.com/Merserk/ComfyUI-PiD) — ⭐ 25 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a custom node for ComfyUI that integrates NVIDIA PiD (Pixel Diffusion) for decoding and upscale workflows, supporting diffusion models like SD3.
- [NyxAwroo/IMG-Dataset-Refiner](https://github.com/NyxAwroo/IMG-Dataset-Refiner) — ⭐ 23 · `Python` · Application — This is an advanced local tool designed for intelligent preparation and selection of graphical data, specifically for training datasets used in LoRAs for image generation models like Flux, Qwen, Z-image, and SDXL.
- [Gavr728/ComfyUI\_KleinTiledUpscaler](https://github.com/Gavr728/ComfyUI_KleinTiledUpscaler) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a ComfyUI plugin providing a highly optimized, self-contained, inpainting-based tiling upscaler node. It is specifically engineered for creative upscaling within the Flux2.Klein diffusion model context.
- [silveroxides/ComfyUI\_SamplingUtils](https://github.com/silveroxides/ComfyUI_SamplingUtils) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides utilities that simplify the configuration of inference parameters, specifically for tasks like mask manipulation and blurring effects within generative AI workflows such as ComfyUI.
- [rudy2steiner/open-prompts](https://github.com/rudy2steiner/open-prompts) — ⭐ 17 · `TypeScript` · Application — This is an open-source web platform designed for discovering, sharing, and reusing AI image prompt templates. It features a curated gallery and a generation studio that supports multiple pluggable providers, facilitating the creation of images within a single workflow.
- [RockeyCoss/LeapAlign\_Code](https://github.com/RockeyCoss/LeapAlign_Code) — ⭐ 16 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository presents 'LeapAlign', a research method from a CVPR2026 paper for optimizing the post-training of flow matching models. It aims to improve image generation quality by addressing challenges in fine-tuning early generation steps of diffusion models.
- [End2End-Diffusion/diffusion-bench](https://github.com/End2End-Diffusion/diffusion-bench) — ⭐ 15 · `Python` · Framework — This project offers a benchmarking and training framework for generative diffusion transformers, simplifying the evaluation of Text-to-Image (T2I) and ImageNet models through flexible configuration.
- [flash-bon/flash-bon](https://github.com/flash-bon/flash-bon) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the official code for Flash-BoN, a method designed to enable instant drafts for inference-time scaling in diffusion models. - [KonokoAz/ComfyUI-Krea2-Reference](https://github.com/KonokoAz/ComfyUI-Krea2-Reference) — ⭐ 12 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This repository provides a workflow or reference implementation for utilizing the Krea2 model within ComfyUI. - [PINTO0309/gazelle-dinov3](https://github.com/PINTO0309/gazelle-dinov3) — ⭐ 80 · `Python` · Paper Code — Gaze-LLE-DINOv3 is an implementation for gaze target estimation, utilizing large-scale learned encoders with DINOv3. It helps verify the provenance and authenticity of images.
- [LukeDitria/mini\_ai\_camera](https://github.com/LukeDitria/mini_ai_camera) — ⭐ 52 · `Python` · Application — This project is a mini AI wildlife monitor built with a Raspberry Pi and IMX500 camera. It performs on-chip inference to identify wildlife, designed for low-power, cloud-free operation.
- [arcships/light-ocr](https://github.com/arcships/light-ocr) — ⭐ 29 · `C++` · Library — This repository provides a C++17 core for the PP-OCRv6 model, designed for Optical Character Recognition. It also includes an asynchronous Node-API adapter, making it easily integrable into Node.js projects.
- [lucasb-eyer/BiternionNet](https://github.com/lucasb-eyer/BiternionNet) — ⭐ 20 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides the code used to reproduce the results from the paper "BiternionNets: Continuous Head Pose Regression from Discrete Training Labels."
- [raphael-baena/morphology4metrology](https://github.com/raphael-baena/morphology4metrology) — ⭐ 12 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository contains the official implementation of an architectural approach for the metrological analysis of historical scripts, utilizing morphological techniques for paleography research.
- [zsh2000/gotohunt](https://github.com/zsh2000/gotohunt) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Paper Code — This repository provides code related to 'Good Token Hunting', a research paper focused on improving token selection methods for Visual Geometry Transformers, enhancing how these models process visual information.
- [malamatenia/morphology4metrology-analysis](https://github.com/malamatenia/morphology4metrology-analysis) — ⭐ 11 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Paper Code — This repository hosts the code for the analysis and experiments detailed in a research paper focusing on leveraging morphology for the metrological analysis of historical scripts.
- [JaceThings/SF-Hello](https://github.com/JaceThings/SF-Hello) — ⭐ 11 · `Python` · Dataset — This repository contains Apple's iconic "Hello" lettering as SVG files, directly extracted from the official Figma design, making them available for design and development projects.
- [rattle99/colorice](https://github.com/rattle99/colorice) — ⭐ 10 · `Python` · Application — Colorice is a tool that generates perceptually accurate color schemes based on a user's wallpaper, designed for 'ricing' desktop environments.

</details>
